sanskrit: आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपोनमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद । विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यंन हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम् ॥
hindiTranslation: मुझे यह बताइये कि उग्ररूपवाले आप कौन हैं हे देवताओंमें श्रेष्ठ  आपको नमस्कार हो ।  आप प्रसन्न होइये ।  आदिरूप आपको मैं तत्त्वसे जानना चाहता हूँ क्योंकि मैं आपकी प्रवृत्तिको नहीं जानता ॥
englishTranslation: Tell me who You are in this fierce form. Salutations to You, best of the radiant beings; be gracious. I wish to know You, the primal one, for I do not understand Your purpose.
essence: Fear bows first, then asks to know what stands before it.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna bows in fear and asks Krishna to reveal who this terrible form is. He wants to understand the primal source behind the overwhelming vision.
simpleInsight: Awe can become clarity when fear turns into honest asking.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna has just seen Krishna's cosmic form devouring the armies. Overwhelmed, he stops resisting, bows, and asks the one question left: who is this fierce presence, and what is its purpose?
modernRelevance: You are staring at a message that changes everything, and your first reaction is fear. Before you can act, you have to ask the honest question: what is really happening here?
translationLiteral: आख्याहि (tell) / मे (to me) / कः (who) / भवान् (You are) / उग्र-रूपः (fierce-formed) / नमः अस्तु (salutations be) / ते (to You) / देव-वर (best of the radiant beings) / प्रसीद (be gracious) / विज्ञातुम् इच्छामि (I wish to know) / भवन्तम् (You) / आद्यम् (the primal one) / न हि (indeed not) / प्रजानामि (I know) / तव (Your) / प्रवृत्तिम् (purpose / action)
insight: It shifts the mind from panic to reverent inquiry before judgment.
